scope.summary: Implement MetaHuman .dna format import: parse the binary DNA file to extract skeleton joint positions and head/body mesh bind poses, generate a ClothBodyProxy capsule chain via joint mapping, store the AVT- + BPX- rows, and flag the garment for MetaHuman-compatible export sizing on promotion
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/tailor/avatars/import/dna accepts a .dna binary file and returns AVT- id
Joint positions from the neutral pose in the DNA file are extracted and passed through JointMapper with the MetaHuman joint-name convention
A valid ClothBodyProxy is produced covering at least the torso, arms, and legs; head is optional
The AVT- row carries source_kind='metahuman_dna' and the DNA file hash for traceability
TailorAvatarCreated + TailorBodyProxyCreated events are emitted; an INFO-level note records that MetaHuman export resizing is the operator's responsibility
cargo test metahuman_dna::test_neutral_pose_joint_extraction passes with a reference .dna fixture
scope.risk_if_missed: MetaHuman is the dominant photorealistic character pipeline for Unreal Engine. Without .dna import, every UE/MetaHuman workflow requires an intermediate FBX export step, breaking the native MD 2025.2 parity that the target audience expects.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
